title: World Food Program USA Zero Hunger Generation Youth Advisory Board 2026 | Volunteer Leadership Opportunity for High School, University & Early Career Youth in the United States
summary: Applications are now open for the Zero Hunger Generation (ZHG) Youth Advisory Board, a national youth leadership opportunity organized by World Food Program USA (WFP USA). This exciting volunteer programme gives young people across the United States the chance to shape national youth engagement strategies, contribute to hunger advocacy initiatives, and collaborate directly with WFP USA staff to strengthen the Zero Hunger Generation movement. The Youth Advisory Board is designed for passionate young leaders who are interested in food security, youth leadership, community impact, social advocacy, fundraising, and public engagement. Selected participants will serve as ambassadors for the Zero Hunger Generation movement while helping to expand youth participation in campaigns focused on ending hunger and advancing global food security. For high school students, university students, and early-career professionals who want to gain national leadership exposure while building real-world advocacy and organizing skills, this opportunity provides a powerful platform for personal growth and meaningful impact. About the Zero Hunger Generation (ZHG) Movement The Zero Hunger Generation initiative is a youth-focused movement supported by World Food Program USA. The initiative seeks to mobilize young people across the United States to engage in advocacy, awareness-building, fundraising, and community action around the issue of hunger and food insecurity. The movement empowers youth to: Raise awareness about global hunger Promote sustainable food systems Lead community initiatives Engage peers in social impact campaigns Support advocacy efforts connected to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) The Youth Advisory Board serves as a core leadership structure within the Zero Hunger Generation ecosystem. What is the Youth Advisory Board? The ZHG Youth Advisory Board is a volunteer leadership body that works directly with World Food Program USA staff to: Strengthen youth engagement nationwide Support movement-building initiatives Expand national outreach efforts Develop youth-led campaigns and programmes Promote innovative ideas for advocacy and engagement Board members play an important role in shaping how young people participate in WFP USA initiatives and national hunger-related conversations. Why This Opportunity Matters Youth participation in global development and humanitarian work is becoming increasingly important. Young people are often closest to the social challenges affecting their communities and can offer creative, innovative, and community-driven solutions. The Youth Advisory Board helps bridge the gap between youth voices and institutional decision-making by: Giving young people leadership opportunities Providing direct collaboration with nonprofit professionals Creating space for youth-led innovation Encouraging peer-to-peer advocacy Building a national network of youth changemakers Participants will gain exposure to nonprofit leadership, youth mobilization, campaign strategy, advocacy work, and public engagement. Benefits of Joining the ZHG Youth Advisory Board Selected participants will gain a wide range of leadership and professional development benefits. 1. Leadership Development Experience Youth Board members will strengthen skills in: Leadership Advocacy Communication Event planning Public speaking Community organizing Team collaboration These experiences can significantly enhance future academic and career opportunities. 2. National Visibility Participants may receive recognition and exposure through: ZHG campaigns WFP USA platforms National youth engagement initiatives Public events and outreach activities This visibility can help participants build strong leadership profiles and expand professional networks. 3. Direct Collaboration with WFP USA Staff Members will work closely with World Food Program USA staff on: Campaign planning Youth outreach Programme development Advocacy initiatives National engagement strategies This provides valuable exposure to nonprofit and international development work. 4. Advocacy and Policy Exposure Participants will engage with: Hunger-related advocacy Youth-driven campaigns Awareness initiatives Community-based solutions This experience is especially valuable for students interested in: Public policy International relations Humanitarian work Social justice Community development 5. Event Leadership Opportunities Board members will have opportunities to: Host virtual events Lead discussions Facilitate youth engagement activities Coordinate community initiatives 6. Networking Opportunities Participants will connect with: Youth leaders nationwide Advocacy professionals Nonprofit experts Community organizers Fellow changemakers Roles and Responsibilities of Youth Advisory Board Members Selected Youth Advisory Board members are expected to actively contribute throughout the programme term. Responsibilities include: Attend Biweekly Meetings Participants must: Attend recurring virtual meetings Commit to approximately 60-minute sessions Participate consistently throughout the one-year term Lead Youth Engagement Activities Members will: Host or lead at least one virtual ZHG event Contribute ideas for campaigns and activities Support movement-building initiatives Support National Recruitment Efforts Participants will help: Expand the ZHG network nationwide Recruit new youth participants Promote youth engagement opportunities Lead Peer-to-Peer Fundraising Members are expected to: Organize fundraising activities Promote awareness campaigns Mobilize community participation Serve as ZHG Ambassadors Participants will represent WFP USA within: Schools Universities Youth communities Local organizations Social media platforms Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all the following eligibility criteria. 1. Be an Active Member of Zero Hunger Generation Applicants must already be active members of the Zero Hunger Generation initiative. Individuals who have not yet joined can sign up HERE 2. Be in Specific Academic or Career Stages Eligible applicants include individuals who are: High school students University students Early-career professionals 3. Commit to the Programme Term Applicants must: Be available for a one-year term Attend recurring virtual meetings consistently 4. Have Parental Consent (If Under 18) Applicants below 18 years old must: Obtain parental consent before participation 5. Be Based in the United States Applicants must: Reside in the United States Be legally eligible to participate in volunteer activities with a U.S.-based nonprofit organization Importantly: Employment authorization is not required Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated by WFP USA staff using a structured assessment process. Candidates will be assessed based on: Commitment to WFP USA’s Mission Applicants should demonstrate: Passion for addressing hunger Interest in advocacy and youth engagement Commitment to social impact Leadership Potential Reviewers will evaluate: Initiative Creativity Communication skills Leadership qualities Professionalism Engagement in ZHG Activities Applicants with prior involvement in: Youth engagement Community service Advocacy Volunteerism may have an advantage. Ability to Implement Ideas Candidates should demonstrate: Problem-solving abilities Action-oriented thinking Organizational skills Campaign or project ideas Selection Process The selection process involves several stages. Stage 1: Written Application Review Applications will first undergo detailed evaluation. Stage 2: Initial Virtual Interview Shortlisted candidates will participate in: A virtual interview with WFP USA staff Stage 3: Final Virtual Interview Finalists will proceed to: A second interview round Successful candidates will then receive final selection notifications. Application Question Example Applicants are asked to provide thoughtful responses to leadership-related prompts such as: “Please suggest one new ZHG activity, incentive, or campaign that would excite young people.” This allows evaluators to assess creativity, innovation, and understanding of youth engagement. Time Commitment Participants should be prepared for: Biweekly virtual meetings Event participation Community outreach Campaign support Leadership responsibilities The programme is designed for young people who can actively contribute while balancing academics or early career commitments. Is the Opportunity Paid? No. The ZHG Youth Advisory Board is a voluntary and unpaid leadership opportunity. However, participants gain: Leadership experience National exposure Advocacy training Networking opportunities Professional development Real-world nonprofit engagement experience These benefits can significantly strengthen future scholarship, internship, fellowship, university, and career applications. Why Young People Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for youth who want to: Build leadership skills Advocate for social causes Gain nonprofit experience Engage in national campaigns Develop professional networks Contribute to ending hunger Represent youth voices nationally The experience can also help participants prepare for future careers in: International development Public policy Humanitarian affairs Community leadership Nonprofit management Advocacy and communications How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the Official Online Application Form provided by World Food Program USA. Applicants should prepare: Personal information Leadership experiences Responses to written questions Community involvement examples Click HERE to learn more about the World Food Program USA Zero Hunger Generation Youth Advisory Board 2026. Conclusion The World Food Program USA Zero Hunger Generation Youth Advisory Board is an outstanding leadership opportunity for ambitious young changemakers in the United States. By joining the programme, participants gain direct exposure to national advocacy work, youth organizing, leadership development, and social impact initiatives while helping shape the future of youth engagement around hunger and food security. For young leaders passionate about creating meaningful change, this programme offers a unique opportunity to build experience, amplify youth voices, and contribute to a national movement focused on ending hunger. Discover more global opportunities on OFY HERE . The post World Food Program USA Zero Hunger Generation Youth Advisory Board 2026 | Volunteer Leadership Opportunity for High School, University Early Career Youth in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Associate Director, Policy at The Centre for Media, Technology and Democracy (CMTD), Canada — Salary Up to $110,000 Plus Benefits
summary: The Centre for Media, Technology and Democracy (CMTD) has announced an exciting opening for the position of Associate Director, Policy, offering experienced policy professionals the opportunity to shape the future of digital governance, AI policy, platform regulation, and democratic resilience in Canada and internationally. Hosted through McGill University and based in Montreal, this high-impact leadership role is ideal for professionals with deep expertise in public policy, media governance, artificial intelligence, online harms, digital sovereignty, and international technology regulation. The successful candidate will work at the intersection of research, policymaking, and public engagement while helping lead one of the most important conversations shaping democracies today — the governance of rapidly evolving digital technologies and AI systems. The position comes with a competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 annually, in addition to benefits, and offers the possibility of renewal after the initial one-year contract. About the Centre for Media, Technology and Democracy (CMTD) The Centre for Media, Technology and Democracy is focused on addressing some of the most urgent challenges facing modern democracies in the digital age. The organization works across research, policy, public engagement, and regulatory development to create evidence-based solutions surrounding: Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance Platform regulation Information integrity Digital sovereignty Online harms Media and democracy Technology governance As AI development accelerates globally, governments and democratic institutions are facing increasing pressure to develop effective regulatory systems. CMTD plays a central role in helping shape these conversations both within Canada and internationally. The Associate Director, Policy will therefore become a key leader within the organization, helping translate complex research into practical policy recommendations that influence governments, regulators, and global institutions. Position Overview The Associate Director, Policy will lead CMTD’s policy stream and oversee the organization’s strategic policy direction. The selected candidate will be responsible for ensuring that the Centre’s research effectively informs public debate, regulatory frameworks, and government decision-making. This role requires someone capable of bridging the gap between academic research and practical policymaking while also managing teams, engaging stakeholders, and representing the organization publicly. The position is full-time and based in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. Contract Duration Initial contract: 1 year Possibility of renewal available Salary and Benefits Annual salary range: $90,000 – $110,000 Additional employee benefits included Salary determined based on experience and educational background Major Responsibilities of the Associate Director, Policy The role includes leadership responsibilities across policy development, stakeholder engagement, research translation, and operational management. Policy Strategy and Research The Associate Director will: Co-lead and shape the Centre’s policy research agenda Develop policy strategies aligned with institutional priorities Focus on major themes such as: AI governance Online harms Platform regulation Information integrity Identify emerging digital policy challenges Coordinate institutional responses to rapidly evolving technology issues Produce and supervise: Policy reports Consultation submissions Policy briefs Opinion editorials (op-eds) Develop integrated approaches connecting: Research Policy design Public engagement Leadership and Team Management Duties The successful applicant will also oversee the operational and management side of the policy stream. Responsibilities include: Supervising policy staff and fellows Mentoring researchers and contractors Setting project deliverables and timelines Creating accountability structures Managing: Budgeting Impact tracking Funder reporting Policy project operations Government and International Engagement One of the most significant aspects of this role involves direct engagement with government officials, regulators, and international organizations. The Associate Director will: Build relationships with: Elected officials Federal and provincial policymakers Parliamentary staff Regulators International governance bodies Represent CMTD in: Parliamentary committee appearances Legislative consultations Government briefings Regulatory proceedings Organize: Expert panels Roundtables Multi-stakeholder commissions Expand the Centre’s role in global technology governance discussions, including participation in: OECD forums United Nations discussions European Union digital policy processes Public Communication and Media Representation The role also demands strong communication and public engagement skills. The selected candidate will: Translate technical and academic research into accessible policy recommendations Represent CMTD in: Conferences Public forums Media interviews Collaborate with research leads and leadership teams to define evidence-based policy positions Required Qualifications Applicants interested in this opportunity must possess strong experience in public policy, governance, and digital technology issues. Required qualifications include: At least five years of experience in: Public policy Policy analysis Government affairs Digital governance AI policy Media policy Platform regulation Experience managing policy professionals or research teams Strong engagement experience with senior policymakers Experience leading: Policy consultations Expert working groups Roundtables Multi-stakeholder processes Excellent communication skills Ability to simplify technical research for policymakers and public audiences A graduate degree (Master’s or equivalent) in: Public Policy Law Political Science Communications Related disciplines Additional Preferred Qualifications While not mandatory, the following qualifications are considered strong advantages: Existing network with: Canadian federal government officials Parliamentary staff International digital policy regulators Familiarity with: EU Digital Services Act OECD AI governance frameworks UN digital governance discussions Bilingual proficiency in English and French Doctoral degree in a related field Who Should Apply? This opportunity is particularly suited for: Policy analysts Government affairs professionals AI governance experts Public policy researchers Digital rights advocates Technology governance specialists Civil society leaders Communications and democracy researchers Candidates who are comfortable operating in both research and policy environments will likely excel in this position. Location and Work Requirements Important details regarding the role include: Position is in-person Based in Montreal, Quebec, Canada Applicants outside Montreal may apply Candidates must be: Authorized to work in Canada Willing to relocate or work on-site in Quebec Application Requirements Applicants must combine all required documents into a single PDF file before submission. The application package must include: Cover letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Writing sample Names and contact details of two references Applicants should note that the Workday application portal only prompts for a CV upload, but all documents must be merged into one PDF file. Important Notes The Workday portal may list the position under a different internal title: “Academic Associate — Policy” However, this is the same role as: Associate Director, Policy at CMTD Deadline No official application deadline has been specified. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This is not a routine policy role. The Associate Director, Policy position offers the chance to directly shape conversations around AI governance, platform accountability, democratic resilience, and technology regulation at a time when these issues are becoming central global policy concerns. Professionals selected for this role will work alongside researchers, policymakers, and international stakeholders helping define the future of democratic governance in the age of AI. Learn More and Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE to learn more about the job opportunity. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Associate Director, Policy at The Centre for Media, Technology and Democracy (CMTD), Canada — Salary Up to $110,000 Plus Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027: Fully Supported Research Residency for International Curators in Tokyo, Japan
summary: The TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for international curators, art critics, and cultural researchers to conduct focused research in Tokyo, Japan. This residency is designed for distinguished and highly motivated professionals working in the fields of curation, art criticism, and cultural research who want to develop new ideas, expand their professional networks, and engage with the artistic and cultural environment of Tokyo. Through this program, selected curators will receive support to carry out independent research, develop their curatorial concepts, and present their ideas in a professional setting. The residency also encourages exchange between international curators and the local creative community in Tokyo. Participants will not only focus on their own research projects but will also contribute to the local arts ecosystem by offering mentoring sessions to emerging creators and delivering a public talk during the residency period. About the TOKAS Curator Residency Program The Curator Residency Program is organized by TOKAS Residency and aims to invite outstanding curators from around the world to Tokyo. The program provides selected participants with time, space, and financial support to pursue research in the areas of contemporary art, curation, art criticism, and cultural studies. This opportunity is especially suitable for curators who are interested in deepening their research, developing new curatorial approaches, and engaging with the cultural landscape of Japan. The residency is not a studio-based program. Instead, it is focused on research, dialogue, mentorship, and public engagement. Curators will be expected to use the residency period to investigate new ideas, connect with relevant cultural practitioners, and present their research outcomes. Residency Location The residency will take place in Tokyo, Japan . Tokyo is one of the world’s most dynamic cultural capitals, offering a rich environment for contemporary art, cultural research, urban studies, design, and interdisciplinary creative practice. For curators and cultural researchers, the city provides access to museums, galleries, artist-run spaces, archives, cultural institutions, and a diverse creative community. Residency Period The TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 will run for 1 to 3 months within one of the following residency periods: From the beginning of May to the end of July 2027 From the beginning of September to the end of November 2027 From the beginning of January to the end of March 2028 Applicants may apply for the available residency periods, but the final placement period will be decided by TOKAS. According to the FAQ, selected applicants are not able to choose the residency period by themselves if multiple periods are selected. TOKAS will make the final decision regarding placement. Number of Curators to Be Selected A total of 6 curators will be selected for the program. The structure is as follows: 2 curators per residency period 6 curators in total This makes the program highly competitive, as it is open to applicants from around the world and only a small number of participants will be chosen. Eligible Disciplines The residency is open to professionals working in the following fields: Curation Art criticism Cultural research The program is particularly relevant for applicants who are actively engaged in contemporary art, cultural discourse, curatorial research, exhibition-making, criticism, or related fields in arts and culture. What the TOKAS Curator Residency Offers Selected participants will receive several forms of support during the residency. These include: 1. Airfare Support The program provides airfare support for selected curators. This helps reduce the financial burden of international travel to Japan. 2. Research or Project Fee Selected participants will receive a fee to support their research or project development during the residency period. This funding is intended to help curators develop and present their ideas while in Tokyo. 3. Living Space TOKAS provides accommodation in the form of a single room for participating curators. It is important to note that the accommodation is only for the selected curator. Participants cannot bring family members or partners to stay with them in the residency accommodation. 4. Research Environment in Tokyo Although the program does not provide a studio or workspace, it offers curators the opportunity to conduct research in Tokyo and engage with the local artistic and cultural environment. Studio or Workspace Availability The program does not provide a studio or workspace. This is important for applicants to understand before applying. The program is designed for curatorial and research-based work rather than studio-based artistic production. Responsibilities of Selected Curators Selected curators are expected to actively participate in the residency and contribute to the local creative community. Their responsibilities include: 1. Conducting Research in Tokyo Participants must focus on developing their research or curatorial project during the residency period. 2. Mentoring Local Emerging Creators Selected curators are required to give mentoring sessions to local emerging creators. This is a key part of the program’s exchange and learning model. 3. Delivering a Public Talk Participants must deliver a public talk during the residency. This gives them an opportunity to share their research, curatorial ideas, and professional experience with a broader audience. 4. Focusing on the Residency Project Curators are expected to focus on their new project during the residency period. If a participant needs to travel outside the residency for research or project-related reasons, they must consult TOKAS in advance. Application Period and Deadline The application period for the TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 is: Opening date: May 19, 2026 Deadline: June 16, 2026, at 18:00 JST Time zone: Japan Standard Time, GMT+9 Applicants should carefully note the time zone and submit their application before the deadline. Late submissions are usually not accepted for competitive residency programs, so applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare all required documents early. How to Apply for the TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 Applicants must complete the application process online. The process includes downloading the official application package, preparing the required documents, and submitting everything through the online submission form. Step 1: Download the Application Package Applicants must download the application package titled: Application Package_Curator2027 This package contains the official application documents and instructions. Step 2: Read the Outline Carefully Applicants must read the program outline carefully before completing the application form. The outline contains important details about eligibility, required documents, residency conditions, and application rules. Step 3: Fill in the Application Form Applicants must complete the official application form in PDF format. The FAQ notes that applicants should use Adobe Reader to fill in the form. The application form has specific settings for font size and character limits. Changing the settings manually may cause text to be cut off or create formatting problems. Step 4: Prepare Supporting Documents Applicants must prepare and upload the following documents: Completed application form in PDF format Two letters of recommendation Portfolio Step 5: Submit Through the Online Submission Form Applicants must fill in their information on the official online submission form and upload the required documents. The online form is titled: Online Submission Form_Curator2027 Letters of Recommendation Applicants are required to submit two letters of recommendation . There is no specific format for the recommendation letters. However, each letter should clearly state: The relationship between the applicant and the referee The reason why the referee recommends the applicant for the program Referees should be specialized in the fields of art and culture. Freelance curators and artists are also eligible to write recommendation letters, as long as the letters explain the relationship with the applicant and the reason for recommendation. Applicants should provide the contact information of the referee’s affiliation. If the referee works as a freelancer, the applicant may provide the referee’s personal contact information. Recommendation letters must be uploaded by the applicant through the online submission form. TOKAS does not accept recommendation letters sent directly by referees. Can Applicants Apply for Other TOKAS Residency Programs at the Same Time? Yes. Applicants may apply for the International Creator Residency Program and/or the Research Residency Program at the same time. However, applicants must check the eligibility requirements for each program and submit separate online submission forms for each residency program. Applicants applying to multiple residency programs may use the same recommendation letters for several programs. Eligibility Information The TOKAS Curator Residency Program does not have an age limit. Applicants who have participated in a TOKAS residency program before may apply again. However, applicants who have already participated in the Curator Residency Program before cannot apply for the same program again. Applicants who are completing a master’s degree may be eligible if: They are not students at the time of participation in the program They have at least 3 years of experience in their specialized area of expertise Applications as a duo are not accepted. Applicants must apply individually. Important Residency Conditions Before applying, candidates should take note of the following conditions: 1. Family or Partners Cannot Join the Residency Accommodation TOKAS provides accommodation only for participating curators. Selected applicants cannot bring family members or partners to stay with them in the provided accommodation. 2. Additional Travel Costs Are Not Covered If a participant travels elsewhere in Japan for research or project purposes, TOKAS will not provide financial support for additional accommodation or travel expenses. 3. Travel Outside Tokyo Must Be Discussed in Advance Curators are expected to focus on their residency project during the program. If they need to stay outside the residency for research or project-related reasons, they must consult TOKAS in advance. 4. Residency Period Is Decided by TOKAS Applicants cannot choose their final residency period after selection. TOKAS will decide and assign the residency period. Inquiry Information Applicants with questions not answered in the FAQ may contact TOKAS through the inquiry form. Inquiry deadline: June 7, 2026 The FAQ will be updated on the TOKAS website after June 11, 2026 . Applicants should note that TOKAS cannot answer each inquiry individually. Why Curators Should Apply The TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 is a valuable opportunity for curators who want to expand their international research practice and engage with Tokyo’s contemporary cultural scene. The program is especially beneficial because it offers: International research experience in Tokyo Financial support through airfare and research/project fee Accommodation in a single room Professional exposure through a public talk Opportunities to mentor emerging creators Time and space to develop new curatorial ideas Access to Japan’s artistic and cultural environment A chance to build international networks in the art and culture sector Final Thoughts The TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027 is a strong opportunity for international curators, art critics, and cultural researchers who are ready to develop new ideas in Tokyo. With support for airfare, project research, and accommodation, the program gives selected participants the resources they need to focus on meaningful curatorial research. Because only six curators will be selected, applicants should prepare a strong application that clearly presents their research interests, curatorial experience, professional background, and motivation for working in Tokyo. Applicants should begin preparing early, especially because the application requires two recommendation letters and a portfolio. The final deadline is June 16, 2026, at 18:00 JST . APPLY HERE. For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here. The post TOKAS Curator Residency Program 2027: Fully Supported Research Residency for International Curators in Tokyo, Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship at SOAS University of London 2026/2027: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for African Students
summary: The Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship at SOAS University of London is now open for applications for the 2026/2027 academic session. This prestigious scholarship is designed to support outstanding students who are passionate about leadership, governance, and Africa’s development. Founded and funded by Dr. Mo Ibrahim, the Mo Ibrahim Foundation is widely recognized for its commitment to strengthening leadership and governance across Africa. Through this scholarship initiative, the Foundation seeks to nurture future African leaders who will contribute meaningfully to the continent’s social, political, and economic transformation. For students seeking a fully funded master’s opportunity in the United Kingdom, this scholarship offers substantial financial support, access to world-class education, and the chance to study at one of the leading institutions for African, Asian, and Middle Eastern studies. Overview of the Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship The scholarship is available for students enrolling in any full-time one-year taught master’s degree programme at SOAS University of London for the 2026/2027 academic year. A total of two scholarships will be awarded, with each scholarship valued at £45,479. The funding package covers: Full tuition fees Maintenance stipend for living expenses Financial support paid directly to the student after tuition deductions The scholarship specifically aims to empower students who demonstrate a strong commitment to governance, leadership, and development issues affecting Africa. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive extensive financial and academic support, including: 1. Full Tuition Coverage The scholarship covers the complete tuition fees for eligible master’s programmes at SOAS University of London. 2. Maintenance Stipend After tuition fees are deducted, the remaining amount from the £45,479 scholarship value will be paid directly to the student to support: Accommodation Feeding Transportation Study materials Daily living expenses 3. Opportunity to Study in the United Kingdom Recipients will gain access to a globally respected educational environment in London, United Kingdom. 4. Access to a Global Academic Network Students will become part of an international network of scholars, researchers, policymakers, and development professionals. 5. Leadership and Development Exposure The programme strongly aligns with African governance and development priorities, helping scholars build expertise that can contribute to positive change across the continent. Eligible Programmes The scholarship applies to: Any full-time one-year taught master’s degree programme Programmes beginning in the 2026/2027 academic session Applicants are encouraged to explore the wide range of postgraduate programmes available at SOAS University of London before applying. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship, applicants must meet all the following requirements: Must be a new admission starting in September 2026 Must have Overseas fee status Must possess a minimum of a 2:1 bachelor’s degree from: A United Kingdom university, or An international equivalent institution Must hold an unconditional offer for an eligible programme before the scholarship panel convenes Must apply for a full-time one-year taught master’s programme It is important to note that: Pre-sessional programmes are not covered under this scholarship Late or incomplete applications will not be considered Preference Criteria Although the scholarship is open to eligible international students, preference will be given to applicants who: Are ordinarily resident in Africa Demonstrate a strong interest in governance in Africa Show commitment to development issues affecting African communities and institutions Applicants should clearly reflect these interests in their personal statements and application materials. How to Apply for the Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship Interested applicants should follow the application process carefully to avoid disqualification. Step 1: Apply for Admission Applicants must first apply for an eligible master’s programme at SOAS University of London. Candidates are advised to apply early because: Admission processing may take up to four weeks During busy periods, processing may extend to six weeks An admission application is required before applicants can access the scholarship application portal. Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship After applying for admission, candidates must submit their scholarship application through the admissions portal. Applicants will need their course application login credentials to access the scholarship application form. Application Deadline The deadline for the Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship application is: 17 June 2026 at 12 noon United Kingdom local time Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit both admission and scholarship applications well before the deadline. Selection Process and Assessment Criteria Applications will be assessed by a scholarship selection panel based on: Academic excellence Personal statement quality Demonstrated commitment to African governance and development Overall strength of application materials The panel will review information submitted in both the admissions application and scholarship application. Important Guidance on Artificial Intelligence Use Applicants are advised not to use generative Artificial Intelligence tools to write their scholarship personal statements. However, Artificial Intelligence tools may be used for: Grammar checking Spelling correction Structuring ideas and outlines Applicants should ensure that all submitted statements genuinely reflect their personal experiences, motivations, and aspirations. Scholarship Notification Timeline The scholarship selection and award process is expected to conclude by mid-July 2026. Successful candidates will be contacted first Unsuccessful applicants will be notified after all awards have been allocated Why This Scholarship Matters The Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship represents more than financial support. It is an investment in Africa’s future leaders, policymakers, and development professionals. Students selected for this programme will have the opportunity to: Gain advanced academic training Develop leadership skills Build international networks Contribute innovative solutions to African governance and development challenges For ambitious students passionate about shaping Africa’s future, this scholarship provides a powerful platform for impact and transformation. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . You may also be interested in these SOAS Scholarships MSc Global Sustainability at SOAS University of London | Climate Change, Sustainable Development and Environmental Policy SOAS University of London Scholarships 2026–27: Over 320 Awards Worth £4 Million Now Opening Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities) Fully Funded SOAS Sustainable Development Goal Scholarships 2026/2027 in the UK | Tuition Fees + £15,000 Stipend The post Mo Ibrahim Foundation Scholarship at SOAS University of London 2026/2027: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme Volunteer Opportunity 2026 for Young Nigerians
summary: Young Nigerians who are passionate about democracy, civic engagement, electoral governance, and public leadership now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience and contribute to strengthening democratic institutions in Nigeria. The EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme (EU-SDGN) is currently recruiting volunteers to support its democratic governance initiatives aimed at promoting electoral integrity, empowering young citizens, and encouraging active participation in governance and nation-building. This opportunity is designed for ambitious young Nigerians who want to build practical experience in governance, policy, civic leadership, electoral processes, and international development while working alongside governance experts and civil society leaders. With democracy playing a critical role in national development, this volunteer programme provides a valuable platform for young people to actively participate in shaping Nigeria’s democratic future. About the EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme (EU-SDGN) The EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme is an initiative focused on strengthening democratic institutions and improving electoral governance in Nigeria. The programme supports civic participation, voter education, electoral integrity, and youth engagement in democratic processes. By recruiting volunteers, the programme aims to empower young Nigerians with the knowledge, exposure, and practical experience needed to become active contributors to democratic governance and civic development. The initiative also reflects the growing importance of youth participation in politics, governance, advocacy, policy development, and democratic accountability. Why This Volunteer Opportunity Matters Across the world, young people are increasingly becoming drivers of democratic change, civic innovation, and policy reform. In Nigeria, youth participation is especially important due to the country’s large youth population and evolving democratic landscape. This volunteer opportunity gives selected participants the chance to: Contribute to meaningful democratic initiatives Support electoral governance projects Participate in civic engagement activities Gain practical field experience Build professional networks Develop leadership and communication skills For young professionals seeking careers in governance, public policy, development work, civil society, law, media, or politics, this programme can serve as an important career launchpad. What Volunteers Will Contribute To Selected volunteers will support a range of activities under the EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme. 1. Planning and Organising Democracy and Governance Events Volunteers will assist in coordinating events related to democracy, governance, and civic participation. This may include: Workshops Public engagements Governance forums Civic education programmes Stakeholder meetings This experience can help volunteers strengthen their event management, communication, and organisational skills. 2. Voter Education and Civic Outreach Campaigns Volunteers will support efforts to educate citizens about: Voting rights Electoral participation Civic responsibilities Democratic engagement Electoral integrity These campaigns are essential for strengthening informed participation in democratic processes across Nigeria. 3. Research and Written Content on Electoral Governance Participants may contribute to: Governance research Policy discussions Electoral analysis Civic education materials Public awareness content This aspect of the programme is especially beneficial for individuals interested in policy research, journalism, governance analysis, law, and advocacy. 4. Youth Political Participation Initiatives Volunteers will support initiatives aimed at encouraging young Nigerians to become more active in: Political participation Civic leadership Community engagement Governance advocacy Democratic reform The programme recognises the importance of youth voices in shaping Nigeria’s future. Who Should Apply? The programme is seeking motivated young Nigerians who are passionate about governance and civic engagement. Ideal applicants include: Students Recent graduates Young professionals Civic advocates Aspiring policymakers Governance enthusiasts Researchers Journalists Law and political science graduates Applicants should demonstrate: Strong communication skills Interest in democracy and elections Commitment to civic participation Professional growth potential Self-motivation and eagerness to learn Fields and Backgrounds That May Benefit Most The opportunity is particularly suitable for individuals with interests or educational backgrounds in: Political Science Law Public Policy Journalism Civil Society Development International Relations Governance Civic Technology Human Rights Development Studies However, applicants from other fields who are passionate about democratic governance are also encouraged to apply. Benefits of the Volunteer Programme The programme offers several professional development opportunities for selected volunteers. Hands-On Experience Participants will gain practical exposure to: Electoral governance Democratic processes Civic engagement Public outreach initiatives Exposure to International Development Work Volunteers will gain insight into: EU-supported governance programming International development initiatives Democracy support mechanisms Civic empowerment strategies Professional Networking Opportunities Participants will have opportunities to connect with: Governance experts Civil society leaders Policy professionals Democracy advocates Development practitioners These connections can help support future career growth and collaboration opportunities. Capacity Building and Mentorship Selected volunteers will benefit from: Workshops Training sessions Professional mentorship Leadership development opportunities These experiences can strengthen participants’ skills and prepare them for future leadership roles. Commitment to Inclusion and Equal Opportunity The programme welcomes applications from all young Nigerians regardless of: Gender Ethnicity Social background Women and applicants living with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply, reflecting the programme’s commitment to diversity, inclusion, and equal participation. How to Apply Interested applicants are required to submit: A current CV A short statement (maximum 300 words) explaining why they want to volunteer Applications should be sent via email with the subject line: “Volunteer Application – Democratic Governance Programme.” Application Email: delegation-nigeria-eusdgn@eeas.europa.eu Application Deadline Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. Final Thoughts The EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme Volunteer Opportunity 2026 is more than a volunteer role. It is a meaningful platform for young Nigerians to actively contribute to democracy, governance, electoral integrity, and civic development. For individuals who aspire to build careers in governance, public policy, civil society, international development, journalism, or advocacy, this opportunity offers valuable practical exposure and professional growth. As Nigeria continues to strengthen its democratic institutions, young people have an important role to play in shaping the nation’s future. This programme provides a pathway for passionate young Nigerians to become part of that transformation. If you are committed to civic leadership and democratic development, this could be the opportunity that helps launch your journey in governance and public impact. For more information, visit the official website. Discover more global volunteer opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post EU Support to Democratic Governance Programme Volunteer Opportunity 2026 for Young Nigerians appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNHCR Innovation Accelerator 2026: Funding, Partnerships, and Global Scaling Support for Humanitarian Innovations
summary: The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) has officially announced the launch of the 2026 UNHCR Innovation Accelerator, a transformative initiative designed to help high-potential humanitarian innovations move beyond pilot stages into sustainable, large-scale impact. The Expression of Interest (EoI) application process will officially open on 1 June 2026 at 10:00 AM CEST , giving organizations worldwide an opportunity to secure strategic support, catalytic funding, mentorship, partnerships, and technical guidance to scale impactful solutions for forcibly displaced and stateless populations. The application deadline is 12 July 2026 at 11:59 PM CEST (Geneva time). The Innovation Accelerator is led by UNHCR Innovation in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders and supported by the Government of Luxembourg. What Is the UNHCR Innovation Accelerator? The UNHCR Innovation Accelerator is a global initiative created to bridge the gap between successful humanitarian pilot projects and sustainable system-wide implementation. Many humanitarian innovations demonstrate strong impact during testing phases but fail to scale because of limited funding, weak institutional support, insufficient partnerships, or lack of long-term implementation pathways. The Accelerator addresses this challenge by supporting tested innovations that already show operational value and community relevance. Its goal is to help these initiatives transition from proof of concept to broader adoption across humanitarian systems and displacement contexts. The initiative is grounded in humanitarian and human rights principles while encouraging future-ready, adaptive, participatory, and ethical solutions. Vision and Mission of the Accelerator The Accelerator aims to strengthen humanitarian responses in a world where displacement crises are becoming increasingly complex and interconnected. As technology, partnerships, financing systems, and service delivery models evolve rapidly, the Accelerator seeks to ensure humanitarian innovation keeps pace with these changes. Its mission includes: Supporting scalable humanitarian innovations Promoting sustainable operational impact Strengthening protection and inclusion for displaced populations Encouraging collaborative humanitarian ecosystems Facilitating institutional adoption of proven solutions Building evidence-driven and community-centered approaches UNHCR emphasizes that transformative humanitarian change cannot happen in isolation. Therefore, the Accelerator strongly promotes partnerships among: Refugee-led organizations Governments UN agencies NGOs Academic institutions Community actors Development partners Private sector stakeholders What Support Will Selected Teams Receive? Selected initiatives will gain access to a comprehensive scaling support system tailored to strengthen sustainability, operational readiness, and long-term impact. 1. Strategy and Governance Support The Accelerator helps organizations refine and strengthen their scaling strategies through: Multi-year programming support Scaling roadmaps Governance structures Objective setting and planning Institutional integration pathways Value proposition refinement Operational delivery planning 2. Partnership Brokering and Ecosystem Navigation Participants will receive support in building strategic partnerships with: Refugee-led organizations Donors and investors Researchers and academic experts Private sector collaborators UNHCR teams and humanitarian actors This collaborative approach helps innovations expand beyond isolated pilots and move toward sustainable implementation. 3. Technical and Operational Guidance Selected teams will access expert technical support to: Improve and adapt solutions Strengthen operational readiness Scale innovations into new contexts Build sustainable implementation systems Improve technical performance and delivery models 4. Learning, Evidence, and Visibility Support The Accelerator places strong emphasis on evidence generation and learning. Participants will receive support in: Monitoring and evaluation Impact measurement Storytelling and communications Capturing operational lessons Knowledge sharing Strengthening visibility and credibility 5. Resource Mobilization Assistance Teams will receive guidance on fundraising and donor engagement, including: Pitch development Partnership presentations Donor mapping Investment readiness Resource mobilization strategies 6. Catalytic Financial Support Selected initiatives may receive catalytic funding to support activities such as: Technical improvements Scaling preparation Testing in new operational contexts Evidence generation Partnership development Operational expansion Funding amounts will depend on the needs and scale of each initiative. Core Principles of the UNHCR Innovation Accelerator The Accelerator operates around three major principles: Diverse Partnerships The initiative encourages collaboration between multiple sectors and organizations to strengthen implementation and scalability. Evidence and Learning Scaling decisions are guided by evidence, operational learning, and measurable impact across different contexts. Sustainability The Accelerator prioritizes long-term impact by supporting innovations capable of sustainable implementation and institutional adoption. Who Can Apply? The Expression of Interest stage is open to organizations and initiatives with proven concepts supporting forcibly displaced and stateless people. Eligible applicants may include: Refugee-led organizations NGOs UN agencies Private sector organizations Universities and research institutions Development partners Community-based organizations Humanitarian consortiums Applicants are strongly encouraged to form collaborative partnerships that combine: Operational expertise Community engagement Technical knowledge Strategic leadership Scaling capacity Multi-Stage Application Process The selection process is designed to identify scalable innovations with strong operational relevance and partnership readiness. Stage 1: Expression of Interest (EoI) At this stage, applicants submit evidence demonstrating: Existing proof of concept Operational testing Scaling ambition Partnership ecosystems Expected impact Strategic relevance Applications will be screened based on: Technical readiness Operational relevance Institutional strength Potential for collaboration Selected applicants may participate in networking and partnership-building activities before moving to the next stage. Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission Shortlisted applicants will be invited to submit detailed Full Proposals. At this stage: Applications must be led by or jointly submitted with UNHCR teams or refugee-led organizations Partnerships between humanitarian and development actors are highly encouraged Applicants must demonstrate operational testing and preliminary results Concept-only proposals are not eligible Teams will present detailed scaling plans covering: Scaling pathways Financial sustainability Risk management Partnership strategies Long-term operational impact Final Selection Shortlisted teams will pitch their initiatives to the Steering Committee. Final selections will be based on: Technical evaluations Scaling potential Strategic relevance Institutional ownership Partnership readiness Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Problem and solution value Proof of concept and evidence Scaling readiness and feasibility Institutional ownership Partnership ecosystem Potential impact at scale Important Dates Expression of Interest Opens: 1 June 2026 at 10:00 AM CEST Application Deadline: 12 July 2026 at 11:59 PM CEST (Geneva time) Why the UNHCR Innovation Accelerator Matters The humanitarian sector increasingly requires scalable, collaborative, and future-focused solutions to respond effectively to displacement crises. The UNHCR Innovation Accelerator offers a rare opportunity for organizations with tested solutions to gain the strategic support, technical expertise, funding, and partnerships needed to scale their impact globally. By connecting humanitarian innovators with refugee-led organizations, governments, academia, development partners, and private sector actors, the Accelerator aims to build more adaptive and sustainable humanitarian systems capable of delivering long-term solutions for displaced populations worldwide. For organizations already working on impactful humanitarian innovations, this initiative could become the catalyst needed to transition from localized success to global operational transformation.
